titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Rebecca Conkling Shelton correspondence with O.B. Ackerley 1990.020
titlestmt.author: Matthew Gorham
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-19 22:32:16 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: Rebecca Conkling Shelton correspondence with O.B. Ackerley
did.abstract: Correspondence between Rebecca Conkling Shelton and O.B. Ackerley concerning Shelton's late husband, the Reverend Frederick William Shelton, and the acquisition of a collection of his sermons.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Source and date of acquisition unknown. The collection was formally accessioned in 1990.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This small collection is housed in a single folder with no particular arrangement.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Rebecca Conkling Shelton correspondence with O.B. Ackerley, 1990.020, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence between Rebecca Conkling Shelton and O.B. Ackerley concerning Shelton's late husband, the Reverend Frederick William Shelton, and the acquisition of a collection of his sermons. The collection also includes a list of Frederick William Shelton's works and a eulogy.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The materials in this collection are in the public domain. While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
